stories.title: Nick Bosa takes victory lap over Ryan Clark’s ESPN layoff
stories.summary: Nick Bosa broke a four-year social media silence with a single emoji, seemingly endorsing Ryan Clark’s firing from ESPN. The 49ers defensive end’s rare post followed Clark’s 2024 criticism of Bosa’s political display during a teammate’s interview. The move has sparked mixed reactions among fans and media personalities.

stories.title: Singer Chris Brown pleads guilty to affray over an altercation at a London nightclub
stories.summary: Grammy-winning singer Chris Brown pleaded guilty Friday to affray over a 2023 London nightclub brawl that injured a music producer. The 37-year-old will be sentenced October 26 after prosecutors dropped more serious charges in exchange for his plea. His co-defendant, vocal coach Omololu Akinlolu, also pleaded guilty to the same charge.

stories.title: The ‘Reacher’ actor is very clear on why he lost the chance to be Thor. It certainly wasn’t because of a lack of muscles.
stories.summary: Alan Ritchson revealed he auditioned for Marvel’s Thor role but lost to Chris Hemsworth. The actor admitted his audition went poorly due to lack of preparation, calling the feedback constructive but harsh. He now stars as Jack Reacher on Amazon Prime Video for three seasons.

stories.title: Jury reaches split verdict in Shiloh Hendrix racial slur case
stories.summary: A jury found Shiloh Hendrix guilty of one count after she allegedly hurled racial slurs at an 8-year-old girl with autism in a Rochester playground on April 28, 2025. Hendrix was acquitted of disorderly conduct but must complete 200 hours of community service, pay a $1,000 fine, and serve supervised probation. The case ignited debates over free speech and protected speech limits.

stories.title: New Jersey Wawa worker died after being run over. Driver loses appeal
stories.summary: A New Jersey teenager lost his appeal against a 10-year prison sentence for running over a Wawa employee who died 50 days later. Bryce Lomas, 17 at the time, pleaded guilty to vehicular homicide and leaving the scene in December 2023. The victim, Andrew Padulano, 60, was struck outside a Wawa store in Old Bridge.

stories.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, and the European Union, with a 10% to 12.5% duty on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. This move is part of an escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump, who claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour. The tariffs will take effect immediately, following the expiration of an identical levy on Friday.

stories.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people are being evacuated from the Cap Ferret peninsula in southwest France and areas near Madrid as wildfires rage across both countries. French authorities have ordered the entire peninsula evacuated, with 10,000 hectares burned, while Spain declares a national emergency as fires worsen near the capital. Firefighters are battling the blazes amid record heat, with officials warning conditions could deteriorate further.

stories.title: Whack-a-drone
stories.summary: The US Federal Communications Commission banned foreign-made drones on December 22, 2025, citing national security risks. Investigators found Odyssey Robot’s Pasadena office in California empty, with no equipment or employees, despite claims of drone manufacturing. The ban now faces scrutiny as critics call it ineffective without verified threats.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, saying current measures under the Renters' Rights Act are already improving the market. She dismissed calls for rent freezes, pointing to Scotland’s expired temporary controls as evidence they do not lower rents. The government will instead focus on empowering renters through existing legislation.

stories.title: New figures show costs rising for pensioners
stories.summary: Pensioners in Jersey are facing rising costs, with heating fuel prices playing a significant role in their inflation measure, contributing to an overall inflation rate of 2.8% in June, up from 2.7% in March. Household services, including a 5.6% price increase, made the largest contribution to inflation, while food prices also rose by 3.5% over the year. The island's inflation rate remains in line with the UK's CPIH measure.

stories.title: Chris Brown pleads guilty to affray over nightclub attack
stories.summary: Singer Chris Brown pleaded guilty to affray for a nightclub attack in London’s Mayfair on 19 February 2023. At Southwark Crown Court on Friday, he entered the plea in under five minutes with sentencing set for 26 October 2026. Brown, known for hits like Loyal and Look At Me Now, arrived flanked by security and waved to cheering fans outside court.

stories.title: Chancellor joins PM on first day working from No 10 North
stories.summary: The UK Prime Minister and Chancellor today opened the new No 10 North office in Manchester, marking a permanent shift in government operations outside London. The hub will host key ministers including Angela Rayner and Yvette Cooper, with plans to expand regional engagement across the UK. The move aims to decentralise decision-making and counter criticism of London-centric governance.

stories.title: Burnham urged to lobby EU leaders directly to waive EES border controls
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is being urged to lobby EU leaders to suspend the new Entry-Exit System (EES) border controls as 2.2 million British holidaymakers prepare to travel abroad this weekend. The Liberal Democrats’ foreign affairs spokesperson, Calum Miller, called for the system to be paused until at least September, citing extreme delays and missed flights. Miller argued that travelers should not be penalized by a malfunctioning system.

stories.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
stories.summary: A South African court has temporarily blocked an impeachment inquiry into President Cyril Ramaphosa over the "Farmgate" scandal. The inquiry was revived after the Constitutional Court ruled in May that parliament had unlawfully shelved a 2022 report suggesting Ramaphosa may have broken the law. The president denies wrongdoing and is separately challenging the panel’s findings in court next month.

stories.title: Why Jimothy the raccoon has become a mega meme online
stories.summary: A Seattle raccoon named Jimothy, believed to have short spine syndrome, has gone viral with over eight million views after a video of it climbing steps was shared on Instagram. The meme has sparked both humor and awareness about spine conditions, with some users hoping Jimothy receives care as he ages.

stories.title: Chris Brown Pleads Guilty Over ‘Vicious and Unprovoked’ Attack at U.K. Nightclub
stories.summary: Chris Brown and his co-defendant Omololu Akinlolu pleaded guilty to affray for a 2023 attack at London’s Tape nightclub. Brown struck the victim twice with a glass bottle to the head while Akinlolu punched and kicked him as he lay defenseless on the floor. Brown was arrested in May 2025 during a U.K. tour stop and now faces sentencing.

stories.title: McClenaghan set to qualify for pommel horse final
stories.summary: Rhys McClenaghan leads the Commonwealth Games pommel horse qualifying with a top score of 14.500 after a flawless routine. The Olympic champion is set to advance to Monday’s final after topping the standings with two subdivisions completed. McClenaghan, returning after shoulder surgery, aims for his third Games medal following gold in 2018 and silver in 2022.

stories.title: Opetaia sues IBF to return cruiserweight world title
stories.summary: Australian boxer Jai Opetaia has initiated a lawsuit in the US District Court for the District of Nevada against the International Boxing Federation, seeking to regain his cruiserweight world title after being stripped of it following a dispute with promotional outfit Zuffa Boxing. The lawsuit claims rival governing bodies, including the World Boxing Council, have conspired to prevent Opetaia from competing, citing instances where they threatened to strip other champions if they faced him. Opetaia is also seeking damages and legal costs, as well as a court order to prevent the IBF from naming another cruiserweight champion while the litigation continues.

stories.title: India’s Paid Music Subscribers Could Hit 30 Million by 2028, EY-IMI Report Finds
stories.summary: India’s paid music streaming subscribers could nearly double to 30 million by 2028, up from 14 million in December 2025, according to an EY and IMI report released Friday. Despite 96% of smartphone owners listening to music daily, only 38% have ever paid for streaming services. The report highlights YouTube as the top platform for music discovery, while dedicated streaming services remain the preferred choice for structured listening.

stories.title: TikTok’s protection of minors should not be opt-in, warns EU
stories.summary: The European Commission has warned TikTok it must strengthen protections for minors or face fines up to 6 percent of global revenue under the Digital Services Act. The EU found teens’ public posts can still be recommended to others and private accounts remain easily discoverable through follower lists. TikTok must adjust default settings so under-18 content is only visible to chosen followers by default.

stories.title: GB Paralympian Schrager given two-year ban after failed drug test
stories.summary: British Paralympic silver medallist Daphne Schrager has been handed a two-year doping ban backdated to June 2025 after testing positive for the muscle-building substance Ligandrol. The 25-year-old, who won silver at Paris 2024, argued environmental contamination but the tribunal rejected her claim. She remains eligible for the 2028 Paralympics but has been omitted from England’s Commonwealth Games squad in Glasgow.

stories.title: South Korea A.I. Billionaire Ordered to Pay More Than Half a Billion Dollars in ‘Divorce of the Century’
stories.summary: A South Korean court awarded $644 million to Roh Soh-yeong, who had sought much more from her ex-husband, Chey Tae-won, the head of the conglomerate behind SK Hynix.

stories.title: Hubble Spies One-Sided Spiral
stories.summary: NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope has captured NGC 4654, a spiral galaxy 72 million light-years away in the Virgo Cluster, showing one side stripped of gas by ram pressure. The galaxy’s lopsided shape results from its high-speed plunge through the cluster’s hot, thin gas. Astronomers will study the stripped tail to learn how such forces shape galaxies over time.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
stories.summary: Thousands of protesters led by India’s online satirical movement, the Cockroach Janta Party, held talks with the government today after a month-long demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation. The government has asked for a day to decide on the resignation while agreeing to compensate families of students who died by suicide after a medical exam was canceled and to drop police cases against marchers. Another nationwide protest is threatened if the minister does not step down soon.

stories.title: Why Trump’s Renewed Trade War Could Be Costly
stories.summary: Households and businesses were already contending with an affordability crunch. A new round of tariffs could inflict more pain.

stories.title: Bangladesh’s President Resigns Amid Uncertainty Over Ex-Leader’s Plans
stories.summary: President Mohammed Shahabuddin stepped down as speculation swirled over the possible return of his onetime ally Sheikh Hasina, who was ousted as prime minister and fled to India.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: US and Iran have engaged in a cycle of retaliatory strikes in the Gulf, with Iran carrying out drone attacks on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East, resulting in at least four deaths and five injuries. The escalation comes as the US plans to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages, a move Iran has condemned as an incendiary precedent. Tensions between the two countries continue to rise, with flights suspended at a major Iraqi airport and sirens sounding in Bahrain and Jordan.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman’s wedding planning triggered a body-loathing spiral, leading to obsessive weighing, calorie counting and binge eating. She lost years of progress after years of casual weightlifting when she tried to shrink for her big day.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: Young people in coastal towns across England and Wales face severe challenges, with youth service funding down 73% in 12 years and nearly a million aged 16 to 24 not in work, education or training. A year-long Guardian project reveals widespread isolation and despair among young residents in 17 towns. The series highlights how decades of neglect have left communities struggling to provide opportunities.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A new play area at Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire aims to educate children about England's rare chalk streams, with over 80% of the world's chalk streams found in the country. The prolonged hot and dry weather is putting pressure on the wildlife that inhabits these unique waterways. The play zone is designed to encourage children to connect with the environment, potentially inspiring a lifelong interest in conservation.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric models cut hospital admissions and missed school or work days by 70% for people with poorly controlled asthma. The project in Cleveland, Ohio, involved 1,200 participants but was halted early due to funding cuts, leaving over 1,100 households without the intervention. Even in the reduced study of 72 homes, the improvements matched or exceeded those from common asthma medications.

offbeat.title: Nick Bosa takes victory lap over Ryan Clark’s ESPN layoff
offbeat.summary: Nick Bosa broke a four-year social media silence with a single emoji, seemingly endorsing Ryan Clark’s firing from ESPN. The 49ers defensive end’s rare post followed Clark’s 2024 criticism of Bosa’s political display during a teammate’s interview. The move has sparked mixed reactions among fans and media personalities.
